questions.q: What does the name Hastinapura mean?
questions.explanation: Hastinapura derives from hastin, the Sanskrit word for elephant, and means the city of the elephant.

questions.q: On which river's banks did Hastinapura stand?
questions.explanation: The Kuru capital stood on the banks of the Ganga, the river whose goddess married King Shantanu.

questions.q: Which dynasty held Hastinapura as its ancestral capital?
questions.explanation: Hastinapura was the seat of the Kuru dynasty, the royal house whose succession disputes drive the epic.

questions.q: Who was king of Hastinapura when the epic's family story begins with a doomed marriage to a goddess?
questions.explanation: King Shantanu of Hastinapura married the river goddess Ganga, beginning the chain of events that leads to the great war.

questions.q: Which catastrophic game was hosted in Hastinapura's assembly hall?
options: The dice game that cost the Pandavas their kingdom
The archery contest for Draupadi's hand
The gambling match of Nala and Pushkara
The riddle contest with the Yaksha
questions.explanation: The fateful dice game against Shakuni took place in Hastinapura's hall, stripping the Pandavas of everything they owned.

questions.q: Which blind king ruled Hastinapura for decades?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra, blind from birth, held the throne of Hastinapura for decades through the rise and fall of his hundred sons.

questions.q: Who was crowned king of Hastinapura after the great war?
questions.explanation: After the victory at Kurukshetra, Yudhishthira was crowned at Hastinapura and ruled for thirty-six years.

questions.q: What rival capital did the Pandavas raise after the kingdom was divided?
questions.explanation: Given the barren half of the realm, the Pandavas built Indraprastha, a capital splendid enough to rival Hastinapura.

questions.q: After which figure is Hastinapura traditionally said to be named?
options: King Hastin, an early ancestor of the Kurus
Queen Hastini, mother of King Kuru
questions.explanation: Tradition credits King Hastin, an early ancestor of the Kuru line, with founding and giving his name to the city.

questions.q: In which modern Indian state does the site of Hastinapura lie?
questions.explanation: Hastinapura is identified with a site in the Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh, near the Ganga.

questions.q: When the kingdom was split, which portion did the Pandavas receive?
options: The fertile lands around Hastinapura itself
Khandavaprastha, the barren tract by the Yamuna
The mountain districts north of the Ganga
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra kept Hastinapura for his sons and gave the Pandavas the wilderness of Khandavaprastha, which they transformed into Indraprastha.

questions.q: What unforgettable event marked the princes' graduation tournament at Hastinapura?
options: Ekalavya's defeat of every Kuru prince
An unknown warrior, Karna, matched Arjuna feat for feat
questions.explanation: At the exhibition of arms, the unknown Karna repeated Arjuna's every feat and was crowned king of Anga by Duryodhana on the spot.

questions.q: Who served the Hastinapura court as its wisest counselor and voice of dharma?
questions.explanation: Vidura, born of Vyasa and a serving maid, counselled the kings of Hastinapura and was regarded as dharma incarnate.

questions.q: Which outrage took place before the elders in Hastinapura's hall after the dice game?
options: The burning of the house of lac
The banishment of Karna from court
questions.explanation: Draupadi was dragged into Hastinapura's assembly and Dushasana tried to strip her while the elders sat silent.

questions.q: Who came to Hastinapura as a final envoy asking only five villages for peace?
questions.explanation: Krishna's peace embassy to Hastinapura asked merely five villages for the Pandavas, and Duryodhana refused even that.

questions.q: Between the reigns of its kings, who repeatedly safeguarded Hastinapura's throne as guardian?
questions.explanation: Barred from ruling by his vow, Bhishma served as guardian and regent of Hastinapura across three generations of kings.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana attempt against the envoy Krishna in the Hastinapura court?
options: To bribe him with the throne of Anga
To challenge him to a duel
questions.explanation: Duryodhana plotted to seize Krishna during the embassy, whereupon Krishna revealed his cosmic form to the court.

questions.q: To which town were the Pandavas sent from Hastinapura, into a trap built of lac?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana had the flammable lac palace built at Varanavata, where the Pandavas were sent under pretext of a festival.

questions.q: Whose hundred sons were born and raised within Hastinapura's palace?
questions.explanation: Gandhari's hundred sons, the Kauravas, were born from a divided mass of flesh and raised in Hastinapura.

questions.q: For how many years did the dice game's sentence exile the Pandavas from Hastinapura's domain?
questions.explanation: The second dice game imposed thirteen years of exile: twelve in the forest and a thirteenth spent undiscovered in disguise.

questions.q: Who described the distant war, blow by blow, to the blind king within Hastinapura's walls?
options: Vidura, reading omens in the sky
Vyasa, composing as the battle raged
Yuyutsu, riding daily from the field
questions.explanation: Vyasa granted Sanjaya divine sight so that he could narrate the entire war to Dhritarashtra inside the palace.

questions.q: Who ruled Hastinapura after the Pandavas renounced the world and walked north?
questions.explanation: When the Pandavas departed on their final journey, Abhimanyu's son Parikshit was crowned king at Hastinapura.

questions.q: When Parikshit was set on Hastinapura's throne, who was crowned at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: The Pandavas installed Vajra, Krishna's great-grandson and a survivor of the Yadava destruction, as king at Indraprastha.

questions.q: Excavations at Hastinapura in the early 1950s, led by B. B. Lal, associated the site with which culture?
questions.explanation: B. B. Lal's excavations linked the Hastinapura site to the Painted Grey Ware culture of the Ganga-Yamuna doab.

questions.q: According to Puranic tradition, why was Hastinapura eventually abandoned by King Nichakshu?
options: A great flood of the Ganga swept the city away
A twelve-year famine emptied the countryside
A curse of Vyasa doomed the royal palace
An earthquake split the city's foundations
questions.explanation: The Puranas record that the Ganga washed Hastinapura away in Nichakshu's reign, and the capital was moved to Kaushambi.

questions.q: By what other elephant-derived names does the epic call Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: The epic also calls the city Gajasahvaya and Nagapura, both names built on words for elephant.

questions.q: How long did Dhritarashtra remain in Hastinapura under Yudhishthira's rule before retiring to the forest?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra lived honored in Hastinapura for fifteen years after the war before leaving for the forest with Gandhari and Kunti.

questions.q: During his peace embassy, where did Krishna choose to dine and lodge in Hastinapura?
options: In a royal guesthouse by the gates
In the humble home of Vidura
questions.explanation: Krishna refused Duryodhana's lavish hospitality and took his simple meal at the house of Vidura instead.

questions.q: Why was Shantanu's eldest brother Devapi passed over for Hastinapura's throne?
options: He was slain in a border war with the Panchalas
He suffered a skin ailment and retired as an ascetic
He was born of a maid and barred by the elders
He refused the throne to study under Parashurama
questions.explanation: Devapi had a skin disease that made him unacceptable as king, and he withdrew to the forest as an ascetic, leaving the throne to Shantanu.

questions.q: Which sole son of Dhritarashtra survived the war to serve the new rulers of Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu, Dhritarashtra's son by a serving woman, crossed to the Pandava side before the war and alone survived to serve the new reign.
